title: Understanding Kosher Certification Symbols
title_zh: 理解犹太洁食认证标志
description: A visual guide to the most common hechsher (kosher certification) symbols and what each one means.
description_zh: 最常见的洁食认证标志(hechsher)及其含义的视觉指南。
description_he: מדריך חזותי לסמלי ההכשר (הסמכת כשרות) הנפוצים ביותר ומה כל אחד מהם אומר.
author: Torah Light Project

historical_context.chinese_dynasty: 汉朝 (Han Dynasty, 206 BCE – 220 CE) and 宋朝 (Song Dynasty, 960–1279 CE)
historical_context.jewish_period: Second Temple Period and Medieval Rabbis (Rashi era)
historical_context.parallel: Both Chinese imperial seals and Jewish kosher certification represent systems of trust and quality control in trade and commerce.

cultural_connections: Imperial seals certifying quality in Han Dynasty China
Guild quality control systems in Song Dynasty
Universal need for trusted certification in trade
key_concepts: Five major agencies: OU, OK, Kof-K, Star-K, cRc
target_audience: Native Mandarin speakers with no prior Jewish knowledge, particularly those in China seeking to understand or keep kosher
learning_objectives: Identify the five major kosher certification symbols
Understand the meaning of D, P, and M modifiers
Know how to verify kosher products using online databases
Recognize reliable vs unreliable kosher symbols
Apply kosher certification knowledge when shopping
